title: Commercial MRO Expansion
entry_mechanism: Saudia Technic expansion plus PSAP-attracted international MRO specialists with EASA Part 145 and FAA Repair Station
executive_summary:
Saudi Arabia's commercial aviation sector is undergoing its most dramatic expansion in history — Saudi Arabian Airlines, Riyadh Air, and Flynas have collectively ordered over 400 narrow and wide-body aircraft. This fleet expansion creates a massive domestic MRO demand that is currently projected to flow largely to foreign maintenance facilities in Dubai, Bahrain, Singapore, and Europe. Node CA-1 targets the expansion of Saudi Arabia's commercial MRO capability to capture the maintenance demand from this fleet expansion in-Kingdom.

The global commercial aviation MRO market was valued at $96 billion in 2024 and is growing at 5.8% CAGR. Saudi Arabia's addressable MRO market — based on its expanding fleet — is estimated at $1.8–2.4 billion per year by 2030. Saudia Technic currently captures approximately $400–500 million of this market. Full-spectrum Saudi MRO capability — heavy maintenance, engine overhaul, component repair, and line maintenance across the Airbus and Boeing fleet — could capture $1.2–1.8 billion per year, making it one of the largest service localisation opportunities in the Saudi industrial strategy.

The expansion of Saudi MRO capability also serves Vision 2030's aviation hub ambition: Saudi Arabia positioned as the MRO centre of excellence for the GCC, attracting work from Emirati, Qatari, and Kuwaiti carriers that currently send work to Dubai and Singapore.

companies.name: Lufthansa Technik
companies.rationale: LHT is the world's leading independent MRO provider and has established MRO joint ventures in multiple countries. LHT's relationship with Saudia Technic is the natural partnership base for capability expansion.
companies.contact: LHT VP MRO Joint Ventures, Middle East

companies.name: Air France Industries / KLM Engineering
companies.rationale: AFI KLM E&M has engine MRO capability (CFM56, LEAP, Trent) and component repair. Active Middle East expansion strategy.
companies.contact: AFI KLM E&M VP International Business Development

companies.name: ST Engineering Aerospace
companies.rationale: ST Engineering operates one of Asia's largest MRO facilities and has established multiple international JVs. Active interest in Gulf MRO market.
companies.contact: ST Engineering VP Commercial MRO, Middle East

companies.name: StandardAero
companies.rationale: StandardAero is the world's largest independent engine MRO provider with CFM56 and LEAP capability directly relevant to Saudi Arabia's narrowbody fleet.

companies.name: Joramco (Alia / Royal Jordanian)
companies.rationale: Joramco has expanded from a Jordan Airlines MRO to a regional third-party MRO. The model is directly relevant to Saudia Technic's expansion path.

partners.name: Saudia Technic
partners.current: Airbus A320 and A330 heavy maintenance; some Boeing 777 capability; EASA Part 145 approval
partners.upgrade: Expand to Boeing 787 and A350 heavy maintenance; add LEAP engine shop (CFM56 to LEAP transition); component repair expansion; LHT technical partnership for quality uplift; 300 additional Saudi technicians
partners.timeline: 24 months to Phase I expanded capability

partners.name: GACA (General Authority of Civil Aviation)
partners.current: Civil aviation regulatory authority; EASA bilateral relationship
partners.upgrade: Streamlined EASA Part 145 approval extension process for Saudi MRO; GACA-EASA bilateral upgrade; Saudi National MRO Strategy implementation oversight

partners.name: Saudi Ground Services Company (SAUDIA Group)
partners.current: Ground handling and line maintenance at Saudi airports
partners.upgrade: Upgrade line maintenance to full A-check capability; EASA Part 145 for line stations; expand to component pooling and exchange

partners.name: TVTC Aviation Academy
partners.current: Aviation technician training; some GACA certifications
partners.upgrade: EASA Part 147 approved training organisation; Airbus and Boeing type-rated maintenance engineer programme; 200 Saudi AME graduates per year pipeline
